display.purpose: Lesson openings and strongest narrative orientation.

display-compact.purpose: Lesson openings on compact viewports without sacrificing hierarchy.

headline.purpose: Scene titles and major conceptual transitions.

body.purpose: Readable lesson explanation and supporting interface prose.

body-small.purpose: Supporting card copy and compact panel explanation.

field.purpose: Structured lesson source and data-entry content.

label.purpose: Status, metadata, measurement, and compact orientation.

micro-label.purpose: Stage kickers and highly compact orientation text.

status-chip.purpose: Fidelity and state chips paired with nearby context.

shadows.name: stage-lift
shadows.purpose: Separate the sticky lesson stage from the reading field.

shadows.name: popover-lift
shadows.purpose: Raise compact transient menus close to their trigger.

shadows.name: side-panel-lift
shadows.purpose: Separate right-edge inspectors and source panels.

shadows.name: drawer-depth
shadows.purpose: Establish the lesson drawer as a modal layer.

shadows.name: dialog-depth
shadows.purpose: Reserve the strongest elevation for blocking dialogs.

shadows.name: mobile-sheet-depth
shadows.purpose: Lift compact bottom sheets above the stage.

motion.name: control-feedback
motion.purpose: Color, border, and background feedback for controls.

motion.name: scene-feedback
motion.purpose: Color, border, and background feedback for lesson scene markers.

motion.name: lesson-scroll
motion.purpose: Guide ordinary lesson navigation without forcing spatial motion.

components.name: Default Control Button
components.description: The restrained baseline action for controls and secondary choices.
components.css: .ds-btn-default { min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 12px; color: var(--muted); border: 1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--control-surface); font: inherit; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms, border-color 150ms, background 150ms; } .ds-btn-default:hover { color: var(--text); border-color: var(--accent-border-strong); background: var(--control-surface-hover); } .ds-btn-default:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Accent Action Button
components.description: A decisive primary action or selected control state.
components.css: .ds-btn-accent { min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 12px; color: var(--text-on-accent); border: 1px solid var(--accent); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--accent); font: inherit; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms, border-color 150ms, background 150ms; } .ds-btn-accent:hover { color: var(--text-on-accent-strong); background: var(--text-link-hover); } .ds-btn-accent:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Lesson Card
components.description: A compact guided-learning entry with title, supporting copy, and metadata.
components.html:
GUIDED LESSON

From retina to V1

Follow the visual pathway through the atlas.
components.css: .ds-lesson-card { display: grid; gap: 12px; padding: 16px; color: var(--text); border: 1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--surface-soft); } .ds-lesson-card h3, .ds-lesson-card p { margin: 0; } .ds-lesson-card p { color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.8125rem; line-height: 1.58; } .ds-card-kicker { color: var(--accent); font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.12em; }

components.name: Dark Text Field
components.description: A high-contrast source or data-entry field within a dark panel.
components.html: <label class="ds-field-label" for="ds-source">LESSON MARKDOWN</label><textarea class="ds-field" id="ds-source" rows="3">title: Visual pathway</textarea>
components.css: .ds-field-label { display: block; margin-bottom: 8px; color: var(--text); font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-field { width: 100%; min-height: 96px; padding: 12px; color: var(--text-input); border: 1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--bg-deep); font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.75rem; line-height: 1.55; resize: vertical; } .ds-field:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Fidelity Status Chip
components.description: A compact factual label for geometry or activity fidelity.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-flex; padding: 4px 7px; color: var(--text-chip); border: 1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius: 999px; font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.625rem; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }

components.name: Brand Navigation
components.description: The compact top-bar home link with an instrumental kicker and current title.
components.html: BRAIN ATLASFrom retina to V1
components.css: .ds-brand { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 13px; color: var(--text); text-decoration: none; } .ds-brand-kicker { color: var(--accent); font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.18em; } .ds-brand:hover { color: var(--text-link-hover); } .ds-brand:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Atlas Stage Header
components.description: The factual frame above the shared anatomical viewport.
components.html:
CURRENT SCENE

Signals reach primary visual cortex
components.css: .ds-stage-header { min-height: 68px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 18px; padding: 12px 16px; color: var(--text); border: 1px solid var(--line-strong); background: var(--surface-stage); } .ds-stage-header p { margin: 0 0 3px; color: var(--dim); font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.625rem; line-height: 1.4; letter-spacing: 0.12em; } .ds-stage-header h2 { margin: 0; font-size: 1.02rem; } .ds-stage-header button { min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 12px; color: var(--text-accent); border: 1px solid var(--accent-border); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--accent-soft); } .ds-stage-header button:hover { color: var(--text); border-color: var(--accent); } .ds-stage-header button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }

narrative.overview:
Brain Atlas is a precise, immersive, evidence-led workspace. Anatomical structure remains the primary visual artifact; the interface surrounds it with dark, quiet instrumentation and clear editorial guidance. Luminous signals indicate state and meaning rather than supplying decoration.

The system balances two modes without changing identity: a focused scientific console for operating the atlas and a spacious reading surface for guided lessons. Layered glass, fine borders, and structural shadows establish depth while restrained typography and compact controls preserve attention for anatomy and evidence. It explicitly rejects both the generic bright SaaS dashboard and decorative neon science-fiction spectacle.
keyCharacteristics: Anatomical Night surfaces frame the rendered model without competing with it.
Neural Cyan marks focus, selection, and primary action with disciplined rarity.
Editorial lesson typography pairs with compact monospace instrumentation.
Controls look compact but retain at least 44 by 44 CSS-pixel interaction targets.
Scientific and rendering colors remain owned by data and renderer metadata, not the interface theme.

rules.name: The Signal, Not Spectacle Rule
rules.body: Use Neural Cyan to communicate focus, selection, or action. Do not spread it across large decorative areas.

rules.name: The Scientific Color Sovereignty Rule
rules.body: Interface tokens never recolor anatomical regions, tract identities, activity channels, or other scientific encodings owned by renderer data.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Use sans serif for explanation and interaction. Use monospace for status, metadata, measurement, and compact orientation labels.

rules.name: The Readability Before Atmosphere Rule
rules.body: Never dim inactive lesson prose; express active scene state through rails, borders, and numbered markers. Keep compact lesson prose at 1rem, balance narrative headings, allow long localized or scientific labels to wrap safely, and use tabular numerals for changing instrument values.

rules.name: The One Stage Rule
rules.body: Reparent and resize the existing stage across modes; never recreate the canvas, renderer, visual system, or control model.

rules.name: The Effective Target Rule
rules.body: Every standalone interaction provides at least a 44 by 44 CSS-pixel target. Inline scientific citations remain ordinary inline links so prose rhythm is not distorted.

rules.name: The Structural Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows explain overlap and modality. Never use them as ornamental glow around routine cards.

rules.name: The Restrained Radius Rule
rules.body: Increase radius with elevation and mobility, not with importance. Do not turn every container into a rounded card.

dos: Do let anatomy and evidence remain the brightest, most consequential content.
Do use Neural Cyan only for focus, selection, links, and primary actions.
Do preserve 44 by 44 CSS-pixel targets for standalone controls and navigation.
Do use monospace for status and metadata while keeping explanation in the sans-serif reading voice; use tabular numerals for changing values and progress.
Do adapt side panels into bottom sheets and keep lesson transport safe-area-aware on compact screens.
Do respect reduced-motion preference by stopping spatial travel while retaining useful color and border feedback.
donts: Don't drift toward a bright generic SaaS dashboard or a decorative neon science-fiction spectacle.
Don't reuse interface tokens for anatomical, tract, activity, or fidelity colors whose meaning comes from data.
Don't add another canvas, renderer, runtime coordinate transform, or competing control system.
Don't cover the 3D stage with decorative badges, gradients, or persistent status chrome.
Don't round every panel, lift every card, or use shadows without an overlap or modality reason.
Don't dim or shrink compact lesson prose to show hierarchy, clip long dynamic headings, or suppress all transitions under reduced motion.
